title: Use Case
description: A concrete situation the product is suited to. Use cases anchor product fit to a shopper's real context — the scenario, who it's for, and what outcome to expect.

scenario.description: Short description of the situation the product fits (e.g., 'weekend trail running in mixed weather').

persona.description: The kind of person the scenario applies to (e.g., 'beginner runner', 'parent with young children').

context.description: Environmental or situational qualifiers — location, season, frequency, conditions.

outcome.description: What the product helps the persona achieve in the scenario.

source.description: Categorical origin of the use-case claim. Agents may weight trust by source. Open vocabulary; clients MUST tolerate unknown values. Well-known values: `manufacturer_spec`, `retailer_test`, `customer_reviews`, `expert_review`, `editorial`, `regulatory`, `ai_generated`.
